old_claim.note: Smokeless Powder provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Smokeless powder improved Maxim-style machine-gun effectiveness and battlefield practicality, but it is better modeled as an accelerant than as the first recoil mechanism's enabling substrate.

source_shape.source_locator: The Power of Recoil section: after describing automatic recoil operation, PBS states that Maxim also developed smokeless powder to maximize the gun's effectiveness.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: PBS ties smokeless powder to maximizing the effectiveness of Maxim's automatic machine gun after explaining the recoil mechanism.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The wording supports an accelerant relationship because smokeless powder improves battlefield usefulness and performance rather than supplying the breech/recoil mechanism itself.

ontology_before: Machine Gun modeled smokeless_powder as a generic enabling prerequisite for the 1884 fully automatic Maxim mechanism.
ontology_after: Machine Gun keeps smokeless_powder as an accelerant for effectiveness and practical deployment while the mechanism is grounded in breech loading and cartridge cycling.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: machine_gun -> smokeless_powder is now an accelerates edge.
would_reject_if: The machine_gun node is later narrowed to smokeless-powder-only battlefield adoption after the initial Maxim mechanism.
A stronger source shows the first effective Maxim recoil mechanism could not function without smokeless powder.
The smokeless_powder node is broadened to include all cartridge propellants rather than low-smoke propellant chemistry.
short_reason: Smokeless powder increased effectiveness, not the core automatic recoil mechanism.
